item.title: Listen: The final moments of CBS News Radio
item.description: On Friday, May 22, 2026, CBS News Radio signed off after nearly a century on the air. Christopher Cruise anchored the final newscast and special report.

item.title: Over 40,000 evacuated in California chemical leak as officials warn tank may explode
item.description: Southern California officials have expanded evacuations in several Orange County cities due to concerns over a leaking toxic chemical tank that they say is "actively in crisis."

item.title: Rubio says Iran imposing tolls on Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable"
item.description: The secretary of state tempered optimism for a deal to end the Iran war, calling Iran's bid to "create a tolling system" in the Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable."

item.title: CBS News Radio signs off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio signed off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.

item.title: Rob Base, rapper on classic hip-hop track "It Takes Two," dies at 59
item.description: Rob Base, the MC whose lyrics lit up the dancefloor classic "It Takes Two" and got countless people moving worldwide, passed away Friday.

item.title: Staten Island shipyard explosion, fire leaves 1 dead, over 30 hurt
item.description: One person died and over 30 people, mostly FDNY members, were hurt in an explosion and fire at a Staten Island shipyard Friday.

item.title: AI is driving more job cuts and weighing on hiring, economists say
item.description: Economists say AI is reshaping the U.S. labor market by suppressing hiring even as overall job losses remain limited.

item.title: Ebola "spreading rapidly" in the Congo with national risk "very high"
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization says Ebola has killed at least 7 people in Congo, but the U.N. agency says it knows the epidemic "is much larger."

item.title: The forecast heading into Memorial Day weekend
item.description: CBS News meteorologist Rob Marciano has the weather forecast as the U.S. heads into Memorial Day weekend.

item.title: The Memorial Day sales, items to look out for
item.description: A recent survey from digital savings platform Retail Me Not found 54% of respondents plan to shop Memorial Day weekend sales this year, up 36% from last year. But the survey found the amount people plan to spend has dropped a whopping 70% from last year. Consumer savings expert Andrea Woroch has more.

item.title: Is the Justice Department independent of the president?
item.description: This week, the Justice Department created a so-called "anti-weaponization fund" as part of a settlement between President Trump and his own federal government over the leaking of his tax returns. The settlement also includes a pledge from the IRS that it will not pursue any legal claims it may have against Mr. Trump, his family and his companies. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: Breaking down this summers' most highly anticipated movies
item.description: Disney's latest addition to the Star Wars franchise, "The Mandalorian and Grogu," is kicking off the summer slate of blockbusters. Some other highly-anticipated movies that will be tested at the box office this summer are Steven Spielberg's "Disclosure Day," "Toy Story 5" and "Supergirl." Lucas Shaw, managing editor for media and entertainment at Bloomberg News, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: SpaceX launches more powerful Super Heavy-Starship rocket
item.description: Perfecting SpaceX's mammoth rocket will be critical to NASA's plans for returning astronauts to the surface of the moon.

item.title: How some U.S. schools are teaching kindness classes
item.description: Kindness 101 is a program that uses the stories CBS News finds in our "On the Road" series to teach kindness and character. The stories are paired with lesson plans created by a nonprofit at Iowa's Drake University called "Character Counts!"

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigning as intel chief, citing husband's cancer diagnosis
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is resigning as the director of national intelligence after her husband was diagnosed with a rare form of bone cancer.

item.title: Pentagon releases more UFO files: "Speechless after these observations"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new batch of 64 files related to UFOs, unveiling a second tranche of records under an executive order by President Trump.

item.title: Some schools are teaching kindness classes: "Makes me want to be a better person"
item.description: Schools teach kids how to read, write and do math. But what about a class on how to be kind? In some places, it is. Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with the story.

item.title: Kyle Busch was coughing up blood a day before his death, 911 call reveals
item.description: Kyle Busch's cause of death has not been given, though his family said earlier the NASCAR icon had been hospitalized with a "severe illness."

item.title: Total cost for a classic barbecue increases by nearly 10%, new report finds ahead of summer
item.description: Millions are planning get-togethers with family and friends this weekend, despite high prices. In this week's "Affordability in America," Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: What's inside the latest UFO file release by the Pentagon?
item.description: The Pentagon released a new batch of UFO files on Friday, including more than 50 previously classified videos and documents related to findings dating from the 1940s to about six months ago.

item.title: CBS News Radio clips of fame
item.description: CBS News Radio is shutting down on Friday after nearly 100 years on the air. In this marathon, Major Garrett ventures into the archives to feature memorable moments from the American institution's storied history.

item.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 50 new UFO videos
item.description: The Defense Department released a second batch of UFO files on Friday, two weeks after its first drop. These are all of the videos included in the latest tranche.

item.title: Trump administration to require most green card applicants to leave U.S. first
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced a sweeping policy designed to make it harder for immigrants already in the U.S. to get permanent residency.

item.title: 5/22: CBS Evening News
item.description: 45 million Americans expected to travel for Memorial Day weekend; new details on the death of NASCAR champion Kyle Busch.

item.title: Judge tosses federal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A federal district judge granted Kilmar Abrego Garcia's motion to dismiss two criminal charges on the grounds his prosecution was vindictive and selective.

item.title: 5/22: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as national intelligence director; President Trump defends "anti-weaponization" fund despite GOP backlash.

item.title: NASCAR world remembers Kyle Busch after sudden death
item.description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch has died at age 41, his family confirmed. CBS News' Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: This factor may determine whether you get rich. You don't control it.
item.description: Landing a good-paying job may not be enough to buy a home. A new study finds family wealth plays an outsized role in who becomes a homeowner.

item.title: Stephen Colbert says farewell to "The Late Show" in heartfelt finale
item.description: Stephen Colbert hosted "The Late Show" for the final time Thursday night as the franchise came to an end after 33 years.

item.title: Voluntary departures hit record high as detained immigrants lose hope
item.description: Vilma Palacios is one of thousands who have given up their immigration cases and voluntarily left the U.S. after being detained. More detainees are opting for voluntary departure than ever before, a CBS News analysis found.
